notes_md:
**đỡ** = to feel *better* or to make something *less intense* (pain, tiredness, problems). It’s about reduction, not full recovery.

It usually goes before an adjective or verb to mean “less” of something.  

It can also mean “to relieve” or “to ease” something (pain, stress), and sometimes “to support/hold up” someone physically.

Close synonym: **bớt** (also “less”), but *đỡ* often feels more like improvement after a bad state. Opposite idea: **nặng hơn / tệ hơn** (worse).

translation.en: I need you to help me.
I need you to help me.

notes:
đỡ = to feel better or to make something less intense (pain, tiredness, problems). It’s about reduction, not full recovery.

It usually goes before an adjective or verb to mean “less” of something.  

It can also mean “to relieve” or “to ease” something (pain, stress), and sometimes “to support/hold up” someone physically.

Close synonym: bớt (also “less”), but đỡ often feels more like improvement after a bad state. Opposite idea: nặng hơn / tệ hơn (worse).
exampleSentences: Em cảm thấy đỡ mệt hơn sau khi nghỉ ngơi một lúc.
Chị uống thuốc rồi nên bây giờ đã đỡ đau đầu.
Anh ấy hỏi mẹ xem bà đã đỡ sau trận ốm chưa.

definitions.vi: Hỗ trợ hoặc giúp đỡ ai đó hoặc cái gì đó.
definitions.en: To support or help someone or something.
definitions.es: Apoyar o ayudar a alguien o algo.

example.vi: Tôi sẽ đỡ bạn khi bạn ngã.
example.en: I will help you when you fall.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I feel better after resting for a while.
She took medicine so now her headache is better.
He asked his mom if she was better after the illness.
